title: db::init_data_dir takes &str instead of &Path
description:
src/db.rs:83 — init_data_dir takes '&str' but immediately converts to Path::new(data_dir). The caller in cli.rs (line 127) has to do path.to_str() to convert PathBuf -> &str, only for init_data_dir to convert it back.

Per Rust standards: 'Take &Path not PathBuf in function parameters unless ownership transfer is intended.' The function should take &Path. This also eliminates the fallible UTF-8 conversion in the caller.

Similarly, db::connect already takes &Path correctly, so init_data_dir is inconsistent with its sibling.

Locations:
- src/db.rs:83 (signature)
- src/cli.rs:124-128 (caller doing unnecessary to_str())
